public_home.title: Game Server Overview
public_home.hero_title: All connected game servers at a glance
public_home.hero_subtitle: Status, RTT, and player activity in one central overview. Sign in to the admin area for management and configuration.

recommendation.online: Status is stable again. Verify if open incidents can be closed and follow-up tasks can be documented.
recommendation.offline: High priority: verify node and endpoint reachability, restart affected services if needed, and keep incident communication active.
recommendation.transition: Status is in transition: observe 1-2 more check cycles and switch to OFFLINE runbook if instability persists.
recommendation.maintenance: Planned maintenance is active: align maintenance window and expected duration with stakeholders, then run recovery checks.

events.global_search_hint: The global search in the header also filters by server, reason, message, channel and policy.

disabled.requires_online: Only possible while server is online

section_descriptions.panels: Manage monitoring source panels including panel-specific integration values.
section_descriptions.servers: Manage hoster presets and defaults for onboarding external servers.
section_descriptions.games: Manage predefined game labels available in server creation.
section_descriptions.nodes: Manage worker nodes including node-specific integration values.
section_descriptions.alerts: Manage alert channels and alert policies in a shared view.
section_descriptions.settings: Global system settings including check-profile defaults for monitoring and workers.
section_descriptions.roles: Manage roles and role assignment rules.

help_short.sync_interval_min: Defines how often panel data is refreshed.
help_short.show_in_dashboard_filters: Controls panel visibility in dashboard filters.
help_short.ptero_panel_url: Base URL of the Pterodactyl panel for API integration.
help_short.ptero_panel_api_key_secret: Full API key, processed in write-only mode.
help_short.ptero_panel_api_key_hint: Masked hint for the currently stored key.
help_short.allow_custom_labels: Allows custom game labels beyond predefined values.
help_short.naming_pattern: Defines the naming convention for new game labels.
help_short.require_published_state: Only published game labels can be selected.
help_short.a2s_integration_enabled: Enables Source Query (A2S) checks for game servers.
help_short.a2s_timeout_ms: Timeout for A2S requests in milliseconds.
help_short.a2s_retry_count: Retry attempts for failed A2S requests.
help_short.auto_discovery: Automatically discovers and registers new nodes.
help_short.ptero_node_panel_id: Defines which panel is used for node validation and import.
help_short.ptero_node_identifier_key: Selects the field used to map nodes.
help_short.ptero_node_identifier_value: Concrete value for the selected identifier field.
help_short.ptero_node_sync_interval_sec: Interval for syncing nodes from Pterodactyl.
help_short.game_icon_upload: Uploads a dedicated icon for this game label.
help_short.game_icon_url: Alternative or additional icon URL for this game.
help_short.game_system_tags: Tags that can be reused system-wide in filters and mappings.
help_short.game_max_players: Default max-player limit for servers of this game.
help_short.game_module_key: Internal module/adapter key used for readout logic.
help_short.game_default_check_profile: Check profile preselected for new servers of this game.
help_short.game_query_timeout_ms: Default timeout for query-based checks of this game.
help_short.game_restart_grace_sec: Grace time after planned restarts for this game before OFFLINE alerts are emitted.
help_short.game_requires_query_endpoint: Enforces a query endpoint for servers of this game.
help_short.strict_schema_validation: Enforces strict schema validation for check profiles.
help_short.allow_profile_cloning: Allows cloning existing check profiles as templates.
help_short.auto_update_enabled: Automatically checks for new releases in the GitHub repository.
help_short.default_timeout_ms: Sets the default fallback timeout for checks.
help_short.worker_check_interval_sec: Defines how often the worker starts a full check cycle.
help_short.worker_check_concurrency: Controls how many servers are checked in parallel by the worker.
help_short.dedupe_window_sec: Deduplicates identical alerts within a time window.
help_short.test_message_enabled: Allows sending test messages to alert channels.
help_short.cooldown_sec: Defines the minimum delay between policy alerts.
help_short.escalation_enabled: Enables escalation behavior for ongoing incidents.
help_short.send_resolved_notifications: Sends a message when incidents are resolved.
help_short.role_change_requires_reason: Requires a reason whenever user roles are changed.
help_short.default_new_user_role: Defines which role new users receive by default.
help_short.role_delegation_enabled: Allows authorized roles to assign roles to others.
help_short.server_default_hoster: Selects the default hoster in external-server onboarding.
help_short.server_default_protocol: Sets the default transport protocol for external servers.
help_short.server_default_query_port_mode: Controls how query-port defaults are prefilled.
help_short.server_default_prefer_a2s: Prefers A2S-focused defaults for compatible hosters.
help_short.server_auto_apply_preset_on_create: Automatically applies the selected preset on create.
help_short.server_preset_key: Technical unique key of the preset.
help_short.server_preset_hoster: Hoster this preset is optimized for.
help_short.server_preset_protocol: Recommended protocol for the game endpoint.
help_short.server_preset_notes: Internal notes or usage guidance for this preset.

help_tooltip.auto_sync: When enabled, panel data is refreshed automatically on a schedule. This reduces manual maintenance and keeps source data consistent.
help_tooltip.sync_interval_min: Defines how often panel data is refreshed via API. Lower values improve freshness but increase panel/API load. Typical values are between 30 and 300 seconds (or 1-5 minutes depending on environment size). Example: 60 seconds for small setups, 180 seconds for larger environments.
help_tooltip.show_in_dashboard_filters: Controls whether panel values are visible as dashboard filter options. Disable this if panels are only internal metadata.
help_tooltip.ptero_panel_url: URL of the target panel (for example https://panel.example.com). You can copy this from the browser address bar in the Pterodactyl admin UI. Enter only the panel base URL, not an /api/... path.
help_tooltip.ptero_panel_api_key_secret: Enter the full API key from Pterodactyl here. The key is stored server-side as a secret and is not shown again in plain text after save. Create/manage keys in Pterodactyl Admin under API/Application Keys.
help_tooltip.ptero_panel_api_key_hint: Shows only a masked hint for the currently stored key. This is for orientation and does not replace the secret input.
help_tooltip.ptero_panel_sync_enabled: When enabled, panel inventory is synchronized on schedule. Disable temporarily during panel maintenance or API incidents.
help_tooltip.allow_custom_labels: Allows free-form game labels in addition to predefined values. For clean data governance, production setups usually keep this disabled.
help_tooltip.naming_pattern: Defines the preferred naming convention for new game labels (for example game-mode-region) to ensure consistent filtering and reporting.
help_tooltip.require_published_state: Restricts server creation to published game label entries only. Draft or incomplete labels remain unavailable for selection.
help_tooltip.a2s_integration_enabled: Enables A2S querying for compatible games to retrieve query-level runtime data.
help_tooltip.a2s_timeout_ms: Maximum wait time per A2S request. Lower values detect failures faster but may increase false negatives on slow links. Example: 1500 ms (default), 2500 ms on high-latency links.
help_tooltip.a2s_retry_count: Number of additional A2S retries after failures. Higher values improve robustness but increase total check duration. Example: 1 for fast feedback, 2-3 for unstable networks.
help_tooltip.auto_discovery: Automatically detects and registers new nodes from imported panel data. Enable for faster onboarding; disable when you want strict manual control.
help_tooltip.healthcheck_interval_sec: Interval for node healthchecks. Lower values detect failures faster but increase check traffic and load. Common starting values are 30-60 seconds. Example: 30s for critical environments, 60s for standard load.
help_tooltip.maintenance_window_enabled: Enables maintenance-window logic for this node. During planned operations alerts can be reduced/suppressed to avoid unnecessary noise.
help_tooltip.ptero_node_panel_id: Selects the panel whose API should validate this node id. Choose the panel where the node actually exists so validation and synchronization stay accurate.
help_tooltip.ptero_node_auto_import: Automatically imports node metadata from Pterodactyl. Source data comes from the panel's node management (name, FQDN/IP, internal node id).
help_tooltip.ptero_node_identifier_key: Defines which attribute is used for matching: name, FQDN, or Pterodactyl node id. Choose the one that is stable and unique in your environment.
help_tooltip.ptero_node_identifier_value: Concrete match value for the selected identifier field. Examples: for 'name' use the panel node name, for 'fqdn' use the hostname, for 'ptero_node_id' use the internal node id from Pterodactyl node details.
help_tooltip.ptero_node_sync_interval_sec: Defines how often node data is synced from the panel. Shorter intervals improve freshness but increase API load. Example: 60s in normal ops, 120s in larger installations.
help_tooltip.game_icon_upload: Upload an icon asset for this game (for example PNG/SVG). This improves recognizability in lists and selectors. In this mock flow the selected filename is stored.
help_tooltip.game_icon_url: Optional icon URL (CDN/asset link) if icons are hosted centrally. Prefer stable URLs to keep UI rendering consistent. Example: https://cdn.example.com/games/enshrouded.png
help_tooltip.game_system_tags: Comma-separated tags (for example survival, pvp, coop) that can be reused across the system. Useful for filtering, routing, and default mappings.
help_tooltip.game_max_players: Default max-player value for this game. Used as a sensible preset when creating new servers with this game label. Example: 10 (Valheim), 64 (shooter), 100 (sandbox).
help_tooltip.game_module_key: Selects the internal readout module. Concept reference: `adapter_key` in `check_adapters` and `check_profiles.rules.checks` (for example `tcp_connect`, `enshrouded_query`, `a2s_query`).
help_tooltip.game_default_check_profile: Defines the default check profile for new servers of this game. Concept reference: `check_profiles` and their `rules`. Example: `default-a2s` for Source-based games, `default-tcp` for pure TCP checks.
help_tooltip.game_query_timeout_ms: Game-specific timeout for query readouts. Lower values fail faster, higher values are more tolerant on slow networks. Example: 1200-1800 ms local, 2500 ms over WAN/cloud.
help_tooltip.game_restart_grace_sec: Defines a per-game restart grace window in seconds. During this period panel-linked restart failures are treated as TRANSITION instead of OFFLINE alerts. This allows slower games to have longer tolerated restart windows (for example 120s, 240s, 420s).
help_tooltip.game_requires_query_endpoint: If enabled, servers of this game must provide a QUERY endpoint. This reduces invalid configurations for query-based modules.
help_tooltip.strict_schema_validation: Validates check profiles strictly against the expected schema, preventing incomplete or malformed profile configurations.
help_tooltip.allow_profile_cloning: Allows existing check profiles to be cloned as templates for new variants, reducing setup time and configuration mistakes.
help_tooltip.auto_update_enabled: Enables automatic version checks against the GitHub repository `AmFearLiath/GameserverMonitor`. Update metadata is stored in system status.
help_tooltip.default_timeout_ms: Global fallback timeout for checks when a profile does not define its own timeout. Helps prevent hanging checks. Example: 1500 ms default, 3000 ms for slower targets.
help_tooltip.worker_check_interval_sec: Interval in seconds for starting new check cycles. Lower values improve freshness but increase worker, database, and target-system load. Example: 30s for tight monitoring, 60s for standard operations.
help_tooltip.worker_check_concurrency: Number of parallel server checks per cycle. Higher values reduce total cycle duration but increase load and potential timeouts. Example: 10 for small setups, 25-40 for larger clusters.
help_tooltip.dedupe_window_sec: Time range in which identical alerts are merged to reduce noise and avoid repeated notifications for the same symptom. Example: 300s (5 minutes) as a practical starting point.
help_tooltip.retry_failed_deliveries: Automatically retries failed alert deliveries to improve reliability during temporary network or provider issues.
help_tooltip.test_message_enabled: Allows sending test messages to alert channels so routing and credentials can be validated without creating real incidents.
help_tooltip.cooldown_sec: Minimum delay between two alerts from the same policy. Helps prevent rapid repeated notifications. Example: 180s for critical systems, 600s for lower-priority services.
help_tooltip.escalation_enabled: Enables escalation paths for ongoing incidents, for example notifying additional recipients after a configured duration.
help_tooltip.send_resolved_notifications: Sends a follow-up message when an incident is resolved, improving operational traceability and closure handling.
help_tooltip.role_change_requires_reason: When enabled, every role change requires a documented reason. This improves traceability and auditability of permission changes.
help_tooltip.default_new_user_role: Defines the default role assigned to newly created users. This keeps initial permissions consistent and safe. Example: `Viewer` as secure default.
help_tooltip.role_delegation_enabled: Allows delegated role assignment by authorized operators. In security-sensitive environments this should be enabled cautiously.
help_tooltip.server_default_hoster: Defines the hoster preselected in the external-server form. Set this to your most frequently used provider. Example: `GPORTAL` for GPortal-heavy environments.
help_tooltip.server_default_protocol: Defines the default protocol used for newly created external servers. Choose UDP or TCP based on game requirements. Example: `UDP` for game query checks, `TCP` for simple port checks.
help_tooltip.server_default_query_port_mode: Controls whether query port is mirrored from game port, optional/manual, or disabled by default. Example: `SAME_AS_GAME` for common setups.
help_tooltip.server_default_prefer_a2s: When enabled, onboarding prefers A2S-friendly defaults where applicable.
help_tooltip.server_auto_apply_preset_on_create: Automatically applies the selected preset during creation to reduce manual setup steps.
help_tooltip.server_preset_key: Unique technical key (for example GPORTAL_DEFAULT) used for API-safe identification.
help_tooltip.server_preset_hoster: Specifies which hosting provider this preset targets. Example: `NITRADO` or `APEX`.
help_tooltip.server_preset_protocol: Defines the recommended network protocol for the primary game endpoint. Example: `UDP` for A2S-based checks.
help_tooltip.server_preset_query_port_mode: Determines how query-port input behaves in onboarding and default filling. Example: `MANUAL_OPTIONAL` when query ports vary by plan.
help_tooltip.server_preset_prefer_a2s: Marks the preset for A2S-first behavior when creating external servers.
help_tooltip.server_preset_notes: Free-text notes for operational context, limitations, or recommended usage.

placeholders.ptero_panel_api_key_secret: Enter a new API key to replace the stored one

notes.ptero_panel_api_key_secret_keep: Leave empty to keep the currently stored API key.

servers_settings.title: Configure hosters/presets
servers_settings.presets_from_db_help: These presets are loaded from the database and can be managed through the entries in the Servers tab.

validation.missing_panel_credentials: Please provide panel URL and API key.
validation.missing_node_identity: Please provide panel and node ID.

alert_form.channel_config_help: For Discord use either a direct webhook URL (https://...) or JSON like {"webhook_url":"https://..."}. Leave empty while editing to keep the existing config.
alert_form.policy_cooldown_help: Minimum delay between two alerts from this policy in seconds.
alert_form.policy_channels_empty: No alert channels found. Create at least one channel first.

external_server.title: Add external server
external_server.description: Create servers without panel integration (for example GPortal) with minimal required fields.

help_short.name: Internal display name for the new server.
help_short.hoster: Defines recommended protocol and port defaults.
help_short.query_port: Optional query port for status/player checks.
help_short.protocol: Transport protocol for the game endpoint.
help_short.game_label: Optional game mapping for filters and defaults.

help_tooltip.name: Free-form display name for dashboard and list views. Use a consistent naming convention such as game-region-instance. Example: `CS2-EU-01`.
help_tooltip.preset: Select a hoster preset to auto-fill common values. Presets are managed in the Servers tab.
help_tooltip.hoster: Hoster choice influences sensible defaults (for example UDP/A2S for GPortal/Nitrado). Use Generic for unknown providers.
help_tooltip.host: Enter the reachable address used for checks. DNS names and IPv4 addresses are both valid. Example: `203.0.113.42` or `mc.example.net`.
help_tooltip.game_port: Required primary game traffic port. Must be between 1 and 65535. Example: `25565` (Minecraft) or `27015` (Source).
help_tooltip.query_port: Optional port for query modules (for example A2S). When omitted, presets may fall back to the game port. Example: `27016` for split query ports.
help_tooltip.protocol: Determines whether the primary endpoint is checked via UDP or TCP. Many game servers use UDP.
help_tooltip.game_label: Optional label used for game classification, filtering, and default profile selection. Example: `Enshrouded` or `CS2`.

settings.title: User settings
settings.description: Security and session defaults for user accounts.

error.api_precondition_failed: Precondition failed or target system is unreachable.
error.api_forbidden: You do not have permission for this action.
error.api_rate_limited: Too many requests. Please try again later.
error.api_upstream_unauthorized: Panel reachable, but API key is invalid (401). Start/stop/log actions require a valid Client API key.
error.api_upstream_forbidden: Panel reachable, but API key lacks required permissions (403). Start/stop/log actions require a Client API key with server access (not only an Application key).
error.api_upstream_unreachable: Panel is unreachable. Please verify URL, port, firewall, and routing from the API server to the target network.
error.api_upstream_timeout: Panel did not respond in time. Please verify reachability and latency.
